behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names the file path docs/contribute/doc-style-guide.md, mentions Docusaurus frontmatter conventions, specifies the sections (tone/voice, formatting, writing practices, code snippets, word list with specific terms like TechDocs/Scaffolder/Software Catalog), references microsite/sidebars.ts for sidebar updates, and mentions AGENTS.md and CONTRIBUTING.md. The test-specific requirements (frontmatter id 'doc-style-guide', title containing 'style' or 'guide', specific section headings,

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes WHAT to create (a style guide with specific sections) and WHERE to put it, but does not specify exact content, exact wording, or line-level changes. A developer must reason about the actual content, frontmatter structure, and sidebar integration details.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for behavioral properties: frontmatter id='doc-style-guide', title containing 'style' or 'guide', presence of a '## Tone' section with friendly/professional keywords, presence of formatting/writing practice concepts, a word list with Backstage terms, and sidebar inclusion. These are flexible enough that many different valid implementations would pass — the tests don't assert exact prose, line positions, or specific helper functions.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Most fail-to-pass tests are essentially text-matching: test_style_guide_covers_tone splits content by '## ' and checks for keywords like 'friendly'/'approachable' in the section; test_style_guide_covers_formatting checks 'backtick' in content.lower(); test_style_guide_covers_writing_practices checks 'active voice' in content.lower(). While some tests use Node.js to parse frontmatter (test_style_guide_exists_with_frontmatter) and validate sidebar structure (test_sidebar_includes_style_guide), the

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub file would fail: test_style_guide_exists_with_frontmatter requires >2000 chars and valid frontmatter with specific id/title; test_style_guide_covers_tone requires a '## Tone' section with specific keywords; test_style_guide_has_word_list requires at least 2 Backstage terms; test_sidebar_includes_style_guide requires actual sidebar modification. An empty or minimal stub cannot satisfy these.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /workspace/backstage (solution/solve.sh is mounted or accessible). The agent could potentially read the hidden test file at tests/test_outputs.py to discover exact assertion strings (e.g., 'doc-style-guide', keyword lists like 'friendly', 'approachable', 'welcoming') and craft content to match. There are no apparent measures preventing the agent from accessing /solution/ or test files in the container.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction requires: tone/voice section, formatting standards, writing practices, code snippet formatting, Backstage terminology word list, sidebar update, and reference updates. Tests cover all of these except code snippet formatting as a distinct check (though it's partially covered under formatting) and the AGENTS.md/CONTRIBUTING.md updates (test_repo_doc_references_valid partially checks CONTRIBUTING.md references). Most explicit requirements are tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section comments ('Fail-to-pass (pr_diff)', 'Pass-to-pass (repo_tests)'), descriptive function names (test_style_guide_covers_tone, test_sidebar_includes_style_guide), and docstrings explaining each test's purpose.

structured_data_schema.reason: The task produces a Markdown documentation file and sidebar config edits, not structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: No typos found in filenames, paths, commands, or variable names in the instruction or test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction describes the task at a high level without naming specific line numbers, exact frontmatter field values, or exact keyword lists that tests check for. It mentions general section topics but doesn't leak the exact test assertions.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The gold solution makes localized edits: adds a section to AGENTS.md, inserts a line in CONTRIBUTING.md, creates a new file, and adds an entry to sidebars.ts. These are bounded, non-contradictory changes.
